scope_boundary: synthetic stable-capability-field record discipline and finite Lean predicates only; no runtime route validity, deployed capability identity, evaluator integrity, authority enforcement, replacement safety, rollback execution, source interpretation, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has a stable-capability-field schema, a protocol fixture, finite StableCapabilityFields Lean predicates, proof adequacy review text that keeps the target narrow, exact source mappings for the Stable Capability Fields chapter, and a synthetic stable-capability-field harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures. Those artifacts make qualification predicates, evidence refs, readiness refs, authority ceilings, route scopes, route permission effects, evaluator independence, review triggers, rollback obligations, default-route blockers, and non-claim boundaries executable at the record level. They still do not establish runtime route validity, deployed capability identity, evaluator integrity, authority enforcement, replacement safety, rollback execution, source interpretation adequacy, or deployed behavior.
required_artifacts: deployed stable-capability-field implementation with real route decisions
runtime trace showing route validity under accepted and rejected field candidates
authority-enforcement trace showing candidates cannot widen authority without governance grant
replacement transaction with real rollback dry run or rollback execution
regression suite over real candidate implementations and prior implementations
source interpretation review for the SCF source mappings
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_stable_capability_fields.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: Stable Capability Fields remains at argument
schema validation checks record shape rather than runtime route validity
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for deployed capability identity, evaluator-integrity, or authority-enforcement claims
replacement safety, rollback execution, source interpretation review, and deployed behavior are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as runtime route-validity evidence
synthetic fixture interpreted as deployed capability-identity evidence
finite Lean stable-capability predicates treated as deployed replacement behavior
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after a deployed stable-capability-field implementation, real route-validity traces, independent evaluator-integrity review, authority-enforcement traces, replacement transactions with real rollback dry runs or rollback execution, regression suites over real implementations, and source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove runtime route validity
schema validation does not prove deployed capability identity
finite Lean predicates do not implement stable capability fields
evaluator-independence strings do not prove evaluator integrity
rollback-obligation strings do not prove rollback execution
no independent runtime, formal-methods, evaluator-integrity, replacement-safety, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent runtime-systems, evaluator-integrity, replacement-safety, formal-methods, reviewer-quality, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no authority-enforcement trace over real candidates
no real rollback dry run or rollback execution
no regression suite over real candidate and prior implementations
non_claims: does not promote Stable Capability Fields above argument
does not prove runtime route validity
does not prove deployed capability identity
does not prove evaluator integrity or authority enforcement
does not prove replacement safety or rollback execution
